rulings.Case Title: LBUBS 2004 C-8 VAN NESS LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, A VS. LSL PROPERTY HOLDINGS VI DE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Other Party Todd Robinette'S Notice Of Hearing Receiver'S Final Account And Report
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 17. Todd Robinette's Notice Of Hearing Receiver's Final Account And Report. Receiver¿s Request for an Order Settling and Approving the Final Account and Report and Discharging the Receiver in all cases except #490868 is granted. The Receiver is also permitted to holdback $50,600 ($50,000 in legal fees and $600 in bank fees) in case #490868 to cover future expenses. A hearing is required on the issue of the $292,785.34 distribution to LNR. The Receiver should be prepared to report to the Court the specific nature and reason for this distribution. The Request that the Court not discharge the Receiver in any of the related receiverships until such time that the Court is prepared to discharge the Receiver in all of the related receiverships is denied. Claimants¿ Motion to Continue the Hearing is denied. The Receiver¿s request for a holdback of $5,600 in each case other than #490868 is denied. The Receiver is to recover his incurred fees from the receivership estates. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS H. BARTNING VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions, Supporting Memo

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT MILENA TALIAN, MISSION BAY HOUSING PTNRS, LP, FILLMORE MARKETPLACE, LP, RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike 2nd amended complaint denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE JOHNSON VS. MILENA TALIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT MILENA TALIAN, MISSION BAY HOUSING PTNRS, LP, FILLMORE MARKETPLACE, LP, RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (Negligent Management) is overruled. Plaintiff was given leave to state what duty is owed and how that duty was breached. Plaintiff has alleged various duties and that they were breached. This is sufficient to survive the Demurrer. Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to state a cause of action and there is no reasonable probability that amendment would cure the defect. Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Breach of Contract) is overruled. Plaintiff was given leave to attach the lease to the complaint and she has done so. Whether management can be bound by the house rules is not something to be determined on Demurrer. Moreover, Plaintiff has alleged at paragraph 67 that the management entered her apartment without reasonable notice. This alone is enough to maintain this cause of action. Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (Violation of Court Stipulation) is sustained without leave to amend, but the factual allegations will be permitted to remain. This is a new cause of action that the Court did not give Plaintiff leave to file. However, the factual allegation shall be allowed to remain as they support other causes of action. Demurrer to the 5th cause of action (Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress) is overruled. Whether the conduct alleged is sufficiently extreme and outrageous to support this cause of action is not properly determined on Demurrer. Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Unlawful Discrimination per 42 USC §§ 3601-3631) is sustained without leave to amend. On her third attempt, Plaintiff has still failed to state any cognizable cause of action for discrimination against these defendants. The action

rulings.Case Title: ALAN K. TOMA VS. VERICREST FINANCIAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANT VERICREST FINANCIAL, INC., THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON AS TRUSTEE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (wrongful disclosure) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must amend to clarify how far long the foreclosure process is or if it has been completed. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (breach of implied covenant) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege the fact regarding the underlying contract or attach a copy of the contract. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Violation of the UCL) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has cited the wrong Civil Code provision regarding negotiating with the lender. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (accounting) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff is not alleging that the defendant owes him money. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH BELT VS. CACH, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 9. PLAINTIFF KEITH BELT DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff¿s Demurrer to Defendant¿s affirmative defenses 1¿8, 11 and 13-15 is overruled. Defendant has plead sufficient facts to support these defenses. Plaintiff¿s Demurrer to affirmative defense 9 is sustained with leave to amend. Defendant does not provide sufficient facts to support this defense and, at best, the facts are unclear. Plaintiff¿s Demurrer to affirmative defenses 10 and 12 are sustained without leave to amend. Both of these are based upon conclusions and not facts. Defendant¿s claim of assumption of risk is not in relation to the amount owed on the credit card but on credit reporting and debt collection statutes and Plaintiff cannot assume the risk of another party violating these statutes. Moreover, the affirmative defense of unreasonable conduct similarly fails because this is not a defense to defendant¿s violation of consumer debt practice laws. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ARROWOOD INDEMNITY COMPANY, AS SUCCESSOR TO FIRE VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 15. PETITIONER ARROWOOD INDEMNITY COMPANY, AS SUCCESSOR Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate. Petitioners Motion for Writ of Mandate is denied. The Commissioner of Insurance¿s records obtained while acting as a liquidator of Fremont¿s estate are not ¿public records¿ within the meaning of the Public Records Act. As a general rule, all public records are subject to disclosure absent an express exception provided by the Legislature. Once such exception is where the agency or public official demonstrates a ¿clear overbalance¿ on the side of confidentiality. First, the Department of Insurance has shown that the Commissioner of Insurance has been acting in a capacity as an appointed conservator over an insolvent insurer which is much different than his role as a public official acting on behalf of the State. In fact this distinct difference in roles has been acknowledged by the Courts. Texas Commerce Bank-El Paso N.A. v. Garamendi (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1234, 1244. As such, he steps into the shoes of that insurer. Second, while there are no cases on point regarding the nature of records held by the Commissioner in this role, the Court finds the Coronado Police Officer¿s Assn. v. Carroll (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1001 particularly instructive. In that case the court reasoned that the Public Defender¿s client database was exempt from disclosure because in representing those clients, the Public Defender was acting as a private attorney, not conducting public business. The Court further reasoned that to hold otherwise would subject a criminal defendant, who could not afford to retain private counsel, to public disclosure of their records while those of a defendant represented by private counsel would be immune from public disclosure of their records. Such would be the case here. The documents Petitioner requests in this situation would not have been available if Fremont were solvent and not placed into liquidation and the Com

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GRAF VS. ADAM BLOMQUIST DBA MAXIMO LIMOUSINE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 10, 2011, line 16. DEFENDANT ADAM BLOMQUIST DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff must file, serve and bring to court its opposition to the demurrer. A hearing is required. =(302/LMG)
